title: gradient.rs: off-by-one in is_expired makes entries expire at exact expiry time
description:
File: rust/lichen-node/src/gradient.rs:240-242

The is_expired function returns true when now_ms equals expires_ms, treating entries as expired AT their expiry time rather than AFTER:

```rust
fn is_expired(expires_ms: u32, now_ms: u32) -> bool {
    now_ms.wrapping_sub(expires_ms) < 0x8000_0000
}
```

When now_ms == expires_ms, wrapping_sub returns 0, and 0 < 0x8000_0000 is true, so the entry is considered expired. This causes premature cache invalidation - entries should remain valid at their exact expiry time and only expire afterward.

Fix: Change condition to `now_ms.wrapping_sub(expires_ms) != 0 && now_ms.wrapping_sub(expires_ms) < 0x8000_0000`
close_reason: Updated is_expired to strict >0 check for elapsed (expires only after deadline). Matches forward_buffer test and RFC timing. Removed outdated comment. Tests pass without warning. Commit c0a4d0f6d.
